title: Which app version a decision belongs to
question: When you release a new version of your app, how do decisions tie to that version?
rationale: Add an optional 'version' tag (e.g. '1.2.0') to a decision, marking the app release it belongs to. When you cut a new release, the decisions made for it carry that tag, so you can group 'By version' and see how the decision set grew release over release. Optional, not required — most decisions don't map to one version, and a mandatory field would rot.

options.label: Optional version tag, reused as a grouping/sort axis

tradeoffs.note: see what was decided for each release — a release-anchored view alongside dates (d7)
reuses the second-dimension grouping already built for d26 — 'By version' is nearly free
optional, so a decision with no version just stays untagged instead of forcing a guess
the audit flow (d34) can read git tags to fill the version when back-filling

options.label: Require a version on every decision

tradeoffs.note: every decision is placeable on a release timeline
a field to set every time that's easy to forget and often doesn't apply — the same rot that parked the review-by date in d14

options.label: No version field — rely on dates only

tradeoffs.note: nothing new to model; dates already give a rough timeline
a date isn't a release — can't answer 'what shipped in v1.2'
